title: Micromeles paucinervia
taxonomy: Micromeles paucinervia belongs to the family Rhamnaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as buckthorns. Within this family, Micromeles paucinervia is classified as a genus of its own, Micromeles. Micromeles paucinervia is native to the Himalayan region and is a deciduous shrub or small tree. It is also known as the Himalayan buckthorn, indicating its close relationship with other buckthorn species. The classification of Micromeles paucinervia places it within the broader context of the Rosales order, a diverse group of flowering plants.

intro: Micromeles paucinervia is a small, deciduous shrub native to East Asia, commonly known as the few-veined hawthorn or few-veined white hawthorn, which belongs to the family Rosaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including hypertension, diabetes, and cardiovascular diseases, due to its potential health benefits in lowering blood pressure and blood sugar levels. The bioactive constituents of Micromeles paucinervia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Micromeles paucinervia is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the production of extracts and capsules, to take advantage of its purported health benefits.
